rationale: The Fed-ECB policy gap (~160bps in USD's favor) remains the dominant structural driver, freshly reinforced by Warsh's hawkish hold at the June FOMC and his reiteration of price-stability primacy at Sintra. On the other side, Eurozone inflation surprised to the downside in June (2.8% vs 3.0% expected) and EZ GDP contracted in Q1 2026, while ECB's Lagarde signaled diminished inflation/growth risks at Sintra — opening the door to further ECB easing. key_drivers: Fed hawkish hold (Warsh) vs ECB at 2.15% — ~160bps rate differential favors USD
EZ inflation undershoot (2.8% Jun vs 3.0% exp) + ECB Lagarde dovish at Sintra
EZ GDP contraction Q1 2026 vs US labor market resilience (JOLTs 7.594M)
EURUSD technically exhausted at 1.1430 resistance after June selloff — limited fresh downside catalyst near-term
US-Iran deal progress cuts USD safe-haven demand, partially offsets USD rate advantage
